definitions.technical: A regulatory approach that states broad goals or principles rather than specific numerical requirements. The comment letter argues that principles-based diversification standards (Option A) cannot be reliably audited in the monthly attestation process because they lack clear quantitative benchmarks.
definitions.lay: A style of regulation that sets general goals instead of specific numbers. For example, saying 'diversify your reserves adequately' instead of 'hold no more than 40% at any one bank.' The comment letter argues this approach makes it impossible for auditors to determine clear compliance.
definitions.parenthetical: (a regulatory approach using broad goals rather than specific numerical thresholds, criticized for audit incompatibility)
definitions.legal_significance: The tension between principles-based and rules-based regulation is central to the NPRM's diversification debate. The comment letter argues that the GENIUS Act's monthly attestation mandate effectively requires quantitative standards because auditors need clear, measurable criteria.
